title: Austrobrickellia bakerianum
intro: Austrobrickellia bakerianum, also known as Baker's Brickellia, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various ailments, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful for wound healing and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Austrobrickellia bakerianum include sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids with antioxidant properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
taxonomy: Austrobrickellia bakerianum belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a genus of flowering plants native to Mexico and Central America. The species is part of the subfamily Carduoideae, which comprises thistle-like and other related plants. Austrobrickellia bakerianum's classification places it within the tribe Cardueae, a group of thistle-like plants. It is further categorized as a member of the tribe's Cardueae tribe's subtribe Carduinae.
